name: Sabri v. United States
summary: The Court upheld the federal bribery statute (Section 666) as applied to a member of the general public who bribed a city official, reasoning it was a necessary and proper means of protecting federal spending interests.
significance: Upheld Congress's power to criminalize bribery of officials in organizations receiving federal funds, even reaching members of the general public, as a valid exercise of the Necessary and Proper Clause. Respondents argue it is distinguishable because the Court said it was not Spending Clause legislation.
